scope: public DCLDE call-level embedding run; 27934 calls x 768 dims; 8 providers; 4 ecotypes
main: Frozen AVES2 embeddings carry measurable killer-whale ecotype acoustic structure, but pooled decodability overstates it due to a recording-site shortcut; site-controlled evaluation separates biological structure from the site effect.

rung1_calltype_discovery.verdict: unsupervised discovery does not recover a repertoire; pooled clusters are site-confounded; real call-type labels required

h5_context_decode_dtag.data_source: open animal-borne DTAG deployments (Salish Sea fish-eating killer whales)
h5_context_decode_dtag.context_label: movement-only (depth + jerk-derived prey capture); independent of decoded audio

multi_context_3way.label_rule: foraging = depth>=30m or jerk prey-capture; travelling/resting split by per-individual ODBA median (movement-only)

calltype_context_selectivity.clustering: KMeans on AVES2 embeddings (silhouette 0.062; soft/continuous repertoire)
calltype_context_selectivity.note: within-individual shuffle null preserves each whale's context marginal; production-side context-specificity

controls_binary.note: echolocation peak energy (>>8 kHz) absent by construction at 16 kHz; signal is call structure, not rate/loudness/biosonar

h5_context_decode_dtag.boundary: context-specific production of communicative calls across more than one movement-defined behavioural context, individual held out; not referential meaning and not a response to a broadcast signal

h6_playback_response.head: H6
h6_playback_response.response_evidence: measurable response to a broadcast signal in naive animals
h6_playback_response.behavioural_source: filatova2011playback (re-analysis of a published conspecific playback experiment)
h6_playback_response.boundary: addresses the response side with prior published playback data (receivers act selectively on a broadcast endogenous signal, naive animals); the behavioural experiment is prior published work re-analysed here, and the response tracks dialect membership, NOT call content

c2_calltype_context_specialization.context_label_source: published field ethograms (ford1989, foote2008, riesch2008, yurk2002), NOT embeddings
c2_calltype_context_specialization.boundary: context-specific PRODUCTION across two functionally distinct contexts (foraging vs social); non-circular (labels from ethograms); NOT a claim of meaning

c2_calltype_multicontext_breadth.analysis: catalogue call-type breadth across canonical behavioural contexts (named-unit level)
c2_calltype_multicontext_breadth.context_axis: behavioural contexts only (activity + social); identity-signalling excluded
c2_calltype_multicontext_breadth.context_label_source: published field ethograms (ford1989, foote2008, riesch2008, yurk2002), NOT embeddings
c2_calltype_multicontext_breadth.boundary: the validated repertoire is documented across SIX functionally distinct behavioural contexts (not just movement states); literature-grounded and non-circular; the chi-square non-uniformity is suggestive only (small n, p=0.086); production-side reading of 'more than one context', NOT a claim of meaning

h7_compositionality_beyond_first_order.boundary: structure BEYOND first order (the 2-back call adds information a first-order Markov model cannot explain) is present in SRKW S-calls (p~1e-3 vs first-order surrogates; candidate phrase S01->S04->S01) but NOT detected in NRKW N-calls (p=1.0); a beyond-first-order combinatorial test, not a re-detection of pairwise structure; NOT semantic compositionality

h8_site_invariance.head: H8 (methods contribution): label-free site-invariance transform for frozen embeddings
h8_site_invariance.pipeline: StandardScaler + PCA(100) + site-nuisance subspace projection + LogisticRegression
h8_site_invariance.boundary: a label-free site-nuisance projection modestly improves cross-site ecotype transfer (e.g., SRKW vs TKW 0.597->0.625) while preserving within-site ecotype; ecotype and recording site are confounded in this corpus (SAR single-provider), so recovery is bounded; NOT a claim of meaning

summary.h1: Pooled balanced accuracy 0.910 (MLP accuracy 0.974) collapses to 0.231 (chance 0.250) under leave-one-provider-out; pooled number is a site-confounded upper bound.
summary.h2: PCA+HDBSCAN ARI 0.043 vs ecotype (p=0.002), above shuffled-label null but small and parameter-sensitive.
summary.h3: Balanced 40-token vocabulary: call co-occurrence is non-random (loss 1.809 vs unigram 3.486) but call order is not (p=0.77).
summary.h4: Provider decodable at 0.948; within-site ecotype discrimination across five providers remains 0.889-0.973 (p=0.005), separating biological structure from the site effect, but cross-site transfer is near chance (0.529): the signal is local.
summary.h5_context_decode_dtag: On an independent DTAG archive, communicative calls predict the caller's movement-defined behavioural context under leave-individual-out: binary foraging/non-foraging at 0.770 balanced accuracy across 22 whales (10,834 calls; null 0.499, p=0.005), and a three-way foraging/travelling/resting contrast at 0.577 across the 20 whales carrying all three contexts (chance 0.333; null 0.355, p=0.005) - communication in more than one behavioural context. The link is call-type-specific (call-type x context Cramer's V=0.40 vs within-individual null 0.08, p<1e-3; every cluster context-enriched), and is not an artefact: call rate alone decodes at 0.536, loudness alone at 0.577, and dropping the 25% most click-like clips leaves 0.749 (clips are 16 kHz, so echolocation peak energy is absent). The context label uses tag depth/acceleration only and the individual is held out, so this is context-specific production of communicative calls, not referential meaning and not a response to a broadcast signal.
summary.legacy_context_heads: Exploratory only and superseded by the DTAG H5 decode for behavioural-context evidence: the earlier Wellard recording-level context heads and a vocal-continuation timing proxy are weak recording-level associations; not main claims and not response evidence.
summary.rung1_calltype_discovery: Unsupervised within-SRKW clustering yields no usable repertoire: pooled clusters are 93% single-provider (site artifact, 82% noise) and a single-provider run collapses to one blob; a validated repertoire requires labelled call types (e.g., Wellard Type C, Dryad).
summary.rung4_sequence_structure: Call streams carry non-random first-order sequential structure: adjacent-call MI 1.91 bits vs order-shuffled null 1.65 (p<0.001), still 1.09 bits after removing 40% repetition, and a held-out bigram beats unigram by 1.74 bits/token. A prerequisite for combinatorial coding, not evidence of meaning.
summary.rung4_validated_calltype_sequence: The same first-order test run on VALIDATED catalogue call types (site held constant, within-recording order-shuffle null) is positive in both resident populations: NRKW (31 N-call types, 5,273 calls) adjacent-pair MI 2.85 bits vs null 1.71 (p=1e-3), 1.60 bits after removing heavy bouting (self-transition 0.92), bigram beats unigram by 2.73 bits/token; SRKW (19 S-call types, 1,553 calls) MI 1.29 vs 0.78 (p=1e-3), self-transition 0.52, bigram gain 0.31 bits/token. This repeats the sequence test on validated biological units; still a prerequisite for combinatorial coding, not evidence of meaning.
summary.rung1_calltype_classification: With validated catalogue labels recovered from the DCLDE per-provider annotations, the full call-type catalogue (8,552 detections encoded with AVES2) is discriminated within a fixed recording site at balanced accuracy 0.709 for 14 SRKW S-call types (chance 0.071, majority 0.278) and 0.968 for 18 NRKW N-call types (chance 0.056, majority 0.390), both permutation p~0.005. Call-type identity also transfers across independent sites: a VFPA-trained SRKW classifier scores 0.636 on SMRU over 5 shared types (chance 0.20; 0.830 over the 4 unambiguous types) - the cross-site control the ecotype boundary failed (0.529). Stereotyped call types are therefore a site-independent acoustic category in both resident populations; this is call-type discrimination, not meaning. Supervised success here also explains the Rung 1 discovery failure: the categories exist, but are too fine for unsupervised recovery from site-confounded embeddings.
summary.h6_playback_response: Response evidence from re-analysis of a published conspecific playback (filatova2011playback): wild killer whales reply vocally to same-pod and not different-pod broadcasts (6/0 vs 0/6 pseudoreplication-controlled, Fisher p=0.00216; 8/0 vs 0/6 across all trials), naive free-ranging animals; and frozen AVES2 recovers the Kamchatka dialect call-type space underlying the response contrast (leave-one-out 1-NN purity 0.439 vs a 0.050 label-shuffle null, p=1e-3, 19 types, 57 exemplars). Corroborated across independent broadcast-response datasets (selbmann2026aversive, bowers2018) and natural vocal matching (miller2004repertoires). The behavioural experiment is prior published work re-analysed here; the response tracks dialect membership, not call content.
summary.c2_calltype_context_specialization: Recovered catalogue call types specialize across two functionally distinct contexts: 72% (13/18) are single-context foraging- or socializing-specialists (specialization index 0.722; disjointness Fisher p=0.069); context labels are from published ethograms, not embeddings (non-circular). Production-side reading of 'more than one behavioural context' at the named-unit level; not a claim of meaning.
summary.c2_calltype_multicontext_breadth: Broadening the named-unit context map beyond the foraging-vs-social axis, the validated catalogue call types are documented across SIX functionally distinct behavioural contexts (foraging, travelling, resting, socializing, greeting/excitement, multi-pod aggregation) from published ethograms - not just movement states: 16 named types, specialization index 0.625 (10/16 single-context). Literature-grounded and non-circular; the chi-square non-uniformity is suggestive only (p=0.086, small n). Production-side reading of 'more than one context (e.g., alarm, mating, foraging)' at the named-unit level; not a claim of meaning.
summary.h7_compositionality_beyond_first_order: Structure BEYOND first order - the combinatorial-coding question the comparative literature asks (sperm-whale codas, bonobo/chimpanzee compositionality) - tested against first-order Markov surrogates that preserve unigram+bigram statistics: positive in SRKW S-calls (second-order information delta 0.645 bits, p~1e-3 vs both global and per-recording surrogates; held-out trigram beats bigram by 0.09 bits/token; candidate 3-call phrase S01->S04->S01 at z=7.95) but NOT detected in NRKW N-calls (delta 0.008, p=1.0, despite heavy bouting). A beyond-first-order combinatorial prerequisite; both populations reported; not semantic compositionality (A+B meaning more than A,B).
summary.h8_site_invariance: A label-free site-invariance transform (StandardScaler + PCA + site-nuisance subspace projection) modestly improves cross-site ecotype transfer (e.g., SRKW vs TKW 0.597->0.625; leave-one-provider-out 4-way ecotype 0.402->0.445 vs a 0.234 permutation null, p=0.005) while preserving within-site ecotype (0.883->0.876 after removing 8 nuisance dims). A methods contribution; recovery is bounded because ecotype and recording site are confounded in this corpus (SAR is single-provider); not a claim of meaning.
